mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks by providing a standardized platform. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes and potential resource overhead for managing the middleware.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64

chmod +x kagenti-cli

sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kagenti CLI binary.
> Make the CLI executable.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using the Kagenti CLI, specifying the desired version and node count.

```bash

kagenti create cluster --name my-kagenti-cluster --version 1.30 --node-count 3 --region us-central1

```

> Create a cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kagenti configure kubeconfig --cluster my-kagenti-cluster

```

> Set up kubeconfig for the created cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up additional configurations such as autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

kagenti delete cluster --name my-kagenti-cluster

```

> Delete the Kagenti namespace and all resources within it.
> Delete the Kubernetes cluster created for Kagenti.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step includes draining nodes if necessary, performing the upgrade, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kagenti upgrade --version v0.5.1

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified node count exceeds available resources in the selected region.

**Fix:**
Reduce the node count or choose a different region with more resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command-line tool is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl and ensure it is in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused' when accessing the Kagenti UI
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kagenti service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Kagenti pods with 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' and troubleshoot any issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized' when accessing the API
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The authentication tokens may be expired or invalid.

**Fix:**
Re-authenticate and obtain a new token using the Kagenti CLI.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent communication protocols and enhanced security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'. All Kagenti services should be running without errors.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
